config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights precisely because once the state or the majority can override them too easily, abuse becomes very likely. From my perspective, the best path is to balance both, but with strong limits on sacrificing individual freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya cenderung melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, karena sejarah kami menunjukkan betapa mudahnya “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan masyarakat itu penting, tetapi menurut saya ia harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, kebebasan beragama, dan proses hukum yang adil. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikorbankan, masyarakat mungkin terlihat lebih tertib, tetapi sebenarnya menjadi lebih rapuh dan kurang bebas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the common interest” can be used to silence minorities or excuse unfair treatment. Society does matter, but it should not automatically outrank the individual, because once that principle is accepted too broadly, people with less power are usually the first to lose. Public order, public health, and national security can justify some limits, but those limits must be narrow, necessary, and accountable. A good society is not one that demands blind sacrifice from individuals; it is one that protects people even when the majority finds that inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Amerika Serikat, saya melihat bahwa ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dijadikan alasan utama, itu sering menjadi pintu masuk bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, terutama terhadap kelompok minoritas atau orang yang tidak populer. Memang ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan nasional yang menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat terbatas, transparan, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan aturan umum. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu yang konsisten, bukan dari pengorbanan hak yang terlalu mudah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement: in Indonesia, “society first” is too often used to pressure individuals, especially minorities, to stay quiet and accept unfair treatment. I do not deny that public health, safety, and order matter, but those are exceptions that must be tightly limited and openly justified, not a blank check for the majority. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then the people with the least power will keep paying the highest price. A strong society is not one that can override individuals whenever it wants; it is one that can protect the common good without crushing personal dignity and freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang otomatis dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya sangat curiga pada argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” karena dalam sejarah kami itu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, terutama terhadap minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer. Memang ada pengecualian seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan, tetapi pembatasan seperti itu harus sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan prinsip umum. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar kuat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi orang dari tekanan mayoritas, bukan yang memaksa individu selalu mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
